Guides

Tournaments & Leagues

Ready to play beyond open play? These explainers cover how tournaments are structured (formats, brackets, and divisions), what DUPR is and how to use it, and how leagues and tours fit together — written for someone entering their first event, not for insiders.

Tournaments & Leagues

Official DUPR vs club ratings: why they differ

How club, league, and self ratings differ from DUPR, why a gap is normal, and which number to trust for which purpose.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Tournaments & Leagues

Why your DUPR is lower than you expected

The common reasons a DUPR rating feels low — reliability, opponent strength, score margins, and inflated self or club ratings — and what to do about it.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Tournaments & Leagues

How to self-rate your pickleball level before you have a DUPR

A practical way to estimate your pickleball level from your shots and decisions, what to look at honestly, and how to confirm it with a real DUPR.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Tournaments & Leagues

What a DUPR 3.0 player looks like — and how 3.0 differs from 3.5

A plain description of the shots and decisions typical of a 3.0 player, how 3.0 differs from 3.5, and how to tell roughly where you sit.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Tournaments & Leagues

How a pickleball tournament works: formats and brackets

A newcomer-friendly explainer of tournament divisions, common formats, and how brackets decide medals.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Tournaments & Leagues

What is DUPR? How the rating system works and how to use it

What DUPR measures, how it differs from self-rating, and how players use it to find fair matches and tournament divisions.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
